If we extrapolate from history it could be something like this:

God: Supreme power, the "real" king, but never intervenes in daily affairs

King: Appointed ruler on behalf of god.  The highest authority in all secular and spiritual matters. However, one person cannot literally run the entire kingdom by himself, so he has to delegate. Duties delegated varied by king.
In Europe, the king usually handled all warfare matters and fought in battle, while his wife/court/vassals/priests/seneschals did all the other stuff

The bureaucracy was often very small and consisted of like one manager and a bunch of tax collectors. Each of the tax collectors would then have armed guards who went around regularly collecting taxes in the form of either labor or goods. In Lemuria's case, the tax is likely to be food + a certain number of days of military service or corvee labor per year
